zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hewitt於1535年5月2日出生在英國艾可格亞特 布羅姆利時，她的父親Sir William Hewett34歲，母親Alice Leveson21歲。她於1566年7月10日與David Stone在英國艾可格亞特 布羅姆利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1582年7月3日在她的家鄉享年47歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hewitt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

from the Middle English personal name Hewet, Huet, Hughet, Howet, pet forms of Hugh (Middle English Hewe, Hue; see Hugh and compare Hew ). The spelling Hughet stood for both Huet and the synonymous Huget; see Huggett . This surname has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

habitational name from Hewitts in Chelsfield or Hewitts in Willesborough (both Kent), both named from Old English hīewett ‘cutting’, denoting a place where trees had been cut down. Here there lived families called de la Hewatte (1270), de la hewett (1301), and atte Hewete (1338). The name may also be topographic for someone who lived in a newly made clearing in a wood.

variants.name: Howatt
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Hewitt  .

variants.name: Hewatt
meanings: English: variant of   Hewitt  .

variants.name: Hewitson
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the personal name Hewet; see Hewitt .

variants.name: Howett
meanings: English (East Midlands): variant of   Hewitt  .

variants.name: Huett
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of   Hewitt  .
German (Hütt): status name for someone living in a hut or owning a small shop, Middle High German hütte, or a habitational name from any of several places called Hütt or Hütte. Compare Hutt .

variants.name: Hewett
meanings: English: variant of   Hewitt  .

variants.name: Howat
meanings: Scottish (southwestern): variant of   Hewitt  .

variants.name: Howitt
meanings: English (East Midlands): variant of   Hewitt  .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

variants.name: Huet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre.
English: variant of   Hewitt  .
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Hue   ‘Hugh’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hewitt was born on 2 May 1535, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, her father, Sir William Hewett, was 34 and her mother, Alice Leveson, was 21. She married David Stone on 10 July 1566, in Great Bromley, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 3 July 1582, in her hometown, at the age of 47.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hewitt(이)가 1535년 5월 2일 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Hewett(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Leveson(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1566년 7월 10일 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 David Stone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1582년 7월 3일 그녀의 고향에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com David Stone em 10 de julho de 1566, em Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hewitt родилась 2 мая 1535 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Hewett было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Leveson, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени David Stone 10 июля 1566 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 3 июля 1582 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 47 лет.
